description: Permissions for the main ThinkingRoot Desktop window. Without an explicit capability file Tauri 2 generates an empty ACL, which silently denies `event.listen('chat-event', …)` and breaks streaming chat. The fs-plugin, shell-plugin, and http-plugin are intentionally NOT enabled: all filesystem / shell / network access flows through Rust-side commands gated by `ensure_under_registered_workspace` + the PermissionsGate (DEFAULT_DENY for ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, browser profiles). Enabling those plugins here would let the frontend bypass that pipeline by talking directly to the plugin. Tauri 2's scope grammar can't express 'only the registered workspace roots' (it requires literal paths at capability-build time), so the Rust-side guard remains the single source of truth.
